patterns_recognized: HARD CAP 7 (pick-side bullpen disadvantage) fires on three GOLD picks today (PIT@NYY G2 gap=+0.0216, NYM@MIL gap=+0.0001, CHW@TEX gap=+0.0060, MIA@HOU gap=+0.0205). The archive has repeatedly flagged the sub-0.02 band as a false-negative source, but the one time (7/20 CHW@TEX) this exact matchup's CAP7 fire was OVERRIDDEN upward on qualitative grounds (citing deGrom as an offsetting ace), it lost 10-3 and deGrom got shelled. Lesson applied today: agree with (DOWNGRADE to) the cap's suppression rather than override, especially on CHW@TEX where the starter (Tyler Alexander, not deGrom) doesn't even offer the same override temptation.

HARD CAP 1 (negative-edge GOLD) fires on PIT@NYY G1 (-1.99pp). The archive shows several recent false negatives on score>=4 pre-cap stacks, but the brain prompt explicitly states this cap is non-negotiable and validated 4-for-5 live -- trusted as-is rather than re-litigated.

Dual-opposing D-grade signals (SP xERA + PQI both against the pick) appear on OAK@ARI, CIN@SEA, LAD@PHI, TB@TOR, DET@CHC. The archive has settled this as a stake-suppression-only signal, never a directional fade -- applied by CONFIRMing SKIP without overriding toward the opposing team on any of these.

CHW@TEX is the same matchup graded two days ago (7/20) with an identical CAP7 fire in the same sub-0.02 band; that override lost badly. No F1*-without-F3 signals appear anywhere on today's slate, so the recently-shaky (4W-2L) starred-F1 auto-downgrade rule was not a factor in any decision today.

PIT @ NYY G1.reasoning: Edge is -1.99pp, triggering HARD CAP 1 (negative-edge GOLD prevention), which collapsed the pre-cap A- stack to B-/score=1. The brain prompt is explicit that CAP 1 is non-negotiable (validated 4-for-5 live) and not to be relaxed even when the underlying F1/SP/PQI/Stage-agreement signals look strong. No live news changes the calculus. Defer to the cap.

PIT @ NYY G2.reasoning: HARD CAP 7 fired on hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.0216, which sits at/above the disputed sub-0.02 false-negative band the archive has flagged repeatedly -- this gap is large enough to be a legitimate fire, not the marginal noise seen in the KC@BAL/MIA@MIL cases. Bubba Chandler (PIT SP) is a rookie-caliber arm which adds opposing-side uncertainty the model may not fully price. Defer to the cap.

BAL @ BOS G1.reasoning: Model's own pipeline already assigns SKIP (stake_mult=0) despite a B-grade score of 2 -- edge is in-band (5.82pp) but the raw tier never elevated past SKIP. No hard cap needed to re-derive here; nothing in recent postgame history or live context argues for elevating a pick the model itself declined to stake.

SF @ KC.reasoning: SF was demoted from DIAMOND to GOLD by the rolling-benchmark guardrail, then HARD CAP 10 zeroed the score (5->0) as a bottom-bucket marginal favorite without a Claude CONFIRM anchor. This is functionally the same setup as 7/20's SF@KC (DIAMOND->GOLD demotion, three-layer suppression), where KC won 4-3 and the suppression was validated. Defer to the caps.

NYM @ MIL.reasoning: HARD CAP 7 fired on hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.0001 -- essentially zero, the most extreme example yet of the disputed sub-0.02 false-negative band. The signal stack underneath (F-signal, SP edge 1.23 agreeing, PQI +19.9, Stage agree) is genuinely strong. However, the 7/20 lesson on this exact matchup (NYM@MIL CAP1 DOWNGRADE that missed a 5-run MIL win) and the CHW@TEX override loss both argue against converting a disputed-cap pattern into a real stake without the threshold fix shipped. Defer to the cap; flag for engineering.

WSH @ COL.reasoning: No starred F1 signal is present today (only F2_xwoba_gap=0.025 and F5_bullpen_gap=+1.07 fire), so the F1*-without-F3 downgrade rule that missed on this exact matchup on 7/20 (WSH won 7-3 after a DOWNGRADE) doesn't apply here -- there's no starred signal to distrust. Edge is in-band (5.32pp), Stage 1/2 agree exactly (delta=0.00), no hard cap fired. Sound pick.

OAK @ ARI.reasoning: D-grade with SP xERA and PQI both opposing the pick, and edge (+17.09pp) well outside the valid [4,15]pp band -- the pipeline already self-graded SKIP. This is the same matchup as 7/20 (ARI GOLD then, correctly DOWNGRADEd on a CAP1-bypass plus Gage Jump-style rookie-SP blindspot; OAK won). Dual-opposing signals are suppression-only, not a fade toward OAK -- hold SKIP.

CIN @ SEA.reasoning: fair_prob=0.397 is below the 0.42 viability floor, SP xERA and PQI both oppose CIN, and Stage 1/2 disagree by 0.23. This is an identical setup to 7/20's CIN@SEA (same pick, same SKIP, same dual-opposing config), where SEA shut out CIN 8-0 and the SKIP was validated as the archive's most emphatic correct call. Hold SKIP.

STL @ LAA.reasoning: Edge (+3.63pp) sits just below the 4pp floor, PQI opposes the pick, and CAP 10's bottom-bucket check applies to a marginal 56.4% favorite. Score is already 0 pre- and post-cap. No independent signal argues for elevating past the model's own SKIP.

MIN @ CLE.reasoning: Same matchup and same result-shape as 7/20: HARD CAP 7 fires on hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.0254, above the disputed sub-0.02 false-negative band (this is a legitimate fire, not the marginal-noise pattern), and CAP 10 independently zeroes the bottom-bucket pick_prob=0.531. On 7/20 this exact matchup's CAP7+CAP10 suppression was validated when CLE routed MIN 13-4. Hold SKIP.

LAD @ PHI.reasoning: prob_ceiling_0.70 flag capped an inflated raw probability (0.8449), Stage 1/2 disagree by a massive 0.6086, SP xERA opposes LAD, and PQI is only marginally supportive. Same matchup and pick as 7/20, where PHI beat LAD 10-7 and the dual-opposing D-grade SKIP was correct. No basis to deviate; hold SKIP.

TB @ TOR.reasoning: D-grade with SP xERA and PQI both opposing TB, edge (+3.73pp) below the 4pp floor. Note this matchup's model pick flipped sides versus 7/20 (TOR was picked then, TB is picked now) -- a reminder the underlying edge is thin and unstable either direction. Dual-opposing remains suppression-only; hold SKIP, no fade toward TOR.

SD @ ATL.reasoning: Edge is trivial (+0.53pp) and HARD CAP 10 zeroes the bottom-bucket marginal favorite (pick_prob=0.539). Same matchup, same pick, and near-identical pick_prob as 7/20's SD@ATL, where the CAP10 suppression on a near-coinflip was validated by a narrow 1-run ATL win. Hold SKIP.

CHW @ TEX.reasoning: HARD CAP 7 fires on hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.0060, structurally identical to 7/20's CHW@TEX, where Claude OVERRODE this exact cap citing deGrom's ace innings as an offsetting factor -- deGrom then allowed 5 ER in 4.2 IP and CWS won 10-3, the slate's only real stake and its worst loss. Today's TEX starter is Tyler Alexander, not deGrom, removing even the (already-discredited) override rationale. Defer to the cap; do not repeat the mistake.

DET @ CHC.reasoning: D-grade, negative edge (-1.88pp), SP xERA and PQI both opposing CHC. Identical matchup and pick to 7/20's DET@CHC, where DET beat CHC 8-6 and the dual-opposing SKIP correctly called the loser. Hold SKIP without treating the opposing signals as a fade toward DET.

MIA @ HOU.reasoning: Double suppression: HARD CAP 7 fires on hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.0205 (above the disputed sub-0.02 band, a legitimate fire) and HARD CAP 10 independently zeroes the bottom-bucket pick_prob=0.540 without a CONFIRM anchor. Two independent caps agreeing is stronger evidence than either alone. HOU has also been a recurring model-favorite loser in recent archive entries (BAL@HOU series, 3 straight losses); treat as added caution even though the opponent differs today.

BAL @ BOS G2.reasoning: Diag CSV shows PENDING_SP_DATA (BOS home starter not yet confirmed with 100+ Statcast pitches). Verified live via statsapi: Boston's game-2 starter is still listed as TBD as of this run, confirming the pending state is accurate rather than a pipeline timing gap. No pick possible.
